title: A Duel with a Hidden Wound
description: On pages 11–13, a duel with a hidden wound as the battle between Dorry and Brogy is poisoned by interference, pushing Luffy and Usopp toward a furious confrontation.
scene: A Duel with a Hidden Wound. The battle between Dorry and Brogy is poisoned by interference, pushing Luffy and Usopp toward a furious confrontation. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: 73,466 fights,

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: ...Draws.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: thud!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Gaba Bababa! Hey, DORRY!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I got some rum from guests!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Excel- Lent! I haven't had rum for so long!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Pour me some, will ya!? Gegya Gyagya!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: GE GYA GYA GYA GYA GYA!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Ah! Could his guests be your crewmates?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: He said his two guests were a long-nosed man and a woman.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: That must be USOPP and NAM!!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: What's with them? They made such a fuss about not leaving the ship, too!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Maybe those two really do like adventures!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Then I guess I should be thanking you two as well for these drinks!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Sorry to change the topic,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: But earlier you said that it takes a year for the log to finish recording on this island.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Is that really true?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Haven't you seen the skeletons of them little humans,

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: lying on the ground around these parts?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Most humans who come here end up dying before their log sets.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Some are eaten by the dinosaurs, others give in to heat or starvation.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: And some even try to attack us...

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: whatever the case, they all end up perishing on this island.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Seems that spending even a single year on this island's just too long for you little humans.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: What do I do... even if I survive on this island for that long...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: who knows what would become of my country during that time...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Yeah, spending a whole year here would get kinda boring.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Isn't there some other way, mister?

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Well, I do have one eternal pose.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: But that only points towards my home Elbaf.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: In a way, you could say that Brogy and I are fighting over this eternal pose.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: You want to try stealing it from us by force?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Nah, that won't do.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: We're not trying to go to Elbaf,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: We just want to go on to the next island.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: Right?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Yes... If we veer off our current course to Alabasta,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Then there's no meaning in sailing at all.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yeah, see?

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on a duel with a hidden wound. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
